meta.professor_methodology: Emphasis on systematic analysis using Ohm's law and Kirchhoff's laws
meta.core_formula: V = IR, P = IV, ΣI_in = ΣI_out, ΣΔV = 0
meta.description: Resistor circuits, Kirchhoff's laws, power calculations, and equivalent resistance

patterns.name: Series and Parallel Combinations
patterns.recognition_signal: Circuit with multiple resistors that can be simplified
patterns.logic: Series: same current, resistances add. Parallel: same voltage, reciprocals add

efficiency_trick.name: Two Resistor Parallel
steps: Use product over sum: R_eq = R₁R₂/(R₁ + R₂)

pitfalls.name: Adding parallel resistances
pitfalls.description: Never add parallel resistances directly - add reciprocals

pitfalls.name: Current/voltage confusion
pitfalls.description: Current same in series, voltage same in parallel

worked_example.title: Equivalent Resistance
worked_example.problem_latex: Find R_eq for 6Ω and 3Ω in parallel, then series with 2Ω

decision_tree.question: Can circuit be reduced by series/parallel?

professor_tips: Redraw the circuit after each simplification
Label all currents with directions (can be arbitrary; negative means opposite)
For power: P = IV = I²R = V²/R - pick the form with known quantities
Always verify your answer makes physical sense

confidence_checklist: I can simplify series and parallel resistor combinations
I know when to use Kirchhoff's laws
I can apply Ohm's law correctly
I understand power dissipation in resistors
